item.title: Find Options
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Among the core-uôÙtilôÙs, find is one of the most useôÙful comôÙmandôÙs. Though I use the baôÙsic funcôÙtionôÙs ôÙmost of the time, find has a wide range of paôÙramôÙeôÙterôÙs, and it comes in handy not onôÙly for findôÙing ôÙfiles, but alôÙso for opôÙerôÙatôÙing a bunch of them at once. Here is a very simôÙple exôÙamôÙple.

ImagôÙine you have to move many files to a diôÙrecôÙtoôÙry, but they all call difôÙferôÙent so a glob is no use, and ôÙmanôÙuôÙalôÙly movôÙing all of them is not an opôÙtion. A posôÙsiôÙble apôÙproach would be to loôÙcate the first of the batch (for exôÙamôÙple ôÙby runôÙning ls -lôÙrth). SupôÙpose the first one of the batch is called /tmôÙp/checkôÙpoint (for this exôÙamôÙple let's asôÙsume the files reôÙside at /tmp).

The comôÙmand would be:

find /tmp -type f -anewer /tmp/checkpoint -exec mv '{}' <target_directory> \;

The -type f part is imôÙporôÙtant in orôÙder not to move the enôÙtire diôÙrecôÙtoôÙry (find onôÙly the files). Then we have the -anewôÙer that reôÙceives a file as a paôÙramôÙeôÙter, and it will filôÙter for those files whose ôÙmodôÙiôÙfiôÙcaôÙtion date is greater than the file used as an exôÙamôÙple (hence, this must be the start of the batch), and ôÙfiôÙnalôÙly the -exôÙec part is inôÙterôÙestôÙing beôÙcause as menôÙtioned at the beôÙginôÙning, it alôÙlows to perôÙforôÙm arôÙbiôÙtrary opôÙerôÙaôÙtions on the group of files (in this case to move them to anôÙothôÙer loôÙcaôÙtion, but other acôÙtions such as modôÙiôÙfiôÙcaôÙtionôÙs, sed, etc. are alôÙso posôÙsiôÙble).

AnôÙothôÙer trait I like about find is that presents a seôÙcure and well-deôÙfined inôÙterôÙface, meanôÙing that inôÙ ôÙsome casôÙes I can first check the reôÙsults priôÙor to exôÙeôÙcute an acôÙtion. For exôÙamôÙple, if we would like to checkôÙ ôÙfor deletôÙing some unôÙnecôÙesôÙsary files:

find . -name "*.pyc"

By isôÙsuôÙing this comôÙmand we list some files to erase. And then we can simôÙply do that by apôÙpendôÙing -delete to the very same comôÙmand.

This is just the tip of the iceôÙberg of the things that are posôÙsiôÙble by means of the find comôÙmand and its varôÙiôÙous opôÙtionôÙs.
